title: English Language Paper 2
subject: English
description: Reading comprehension, grammar, punctuation, and writing

passage.title: Back to School
passage.content:
In September, Janelle goes back to school. She wakes up early, makes her bed and eats breakfast. Her mother gives her a cup of tea and a warm patty.

Janelle packs her bag with her books, pencils and a new crayon set. She feels happy because she will see her friends again.

When she reaches school, she says, "Good morning, Miss Brown," and sits quietly in her Grade 2 class. Janelle is ready to work hard this term.

sections.name: Section A – Reading Comprehension
sections.instruction: Read the passage about Janelle and answer the questions.

questions.prompt: What is the girl's name?
questions.hint: Look at the first sentence of the passage.

questions.prompt: In which month does Janelle go back to school?
questions.hint: Check the very first sentence!

questions.prompt: What does her mother give her for breakfast?
options: A cup of tea and a warm patty
questions.correct: A cup of tea and a warm patty
questions.hint: Read the third sentence carefully.

questions.prompt: Name TWO things Janelle puts in her bag:
questions.hint: Read the second paragraph. What does she pack?

questions.prompt: How does Janelle feel about going back to school?
questions.hint: The passage tells us how she feels about seeing her friends.

sections.name: Section B – Nouns (Naming Words)
sections.instruction: Find the nouns (naming words) in the sentences.

questions.prompt: Select the NOUNS in this sentence: 'The boy carries a bag to school.'
questions.hint: Nouns are names of people, places, or things.

questions.prompt: Which word is a PERSON?
questions.hint: A person is someone you can talk to!

sections.name: Section B – Verbs (Doing Words)
sections.instruction: Find the verbs (doing/action words) in each sentence.

questions.prompt: What is the VERB in: 'The dog runs fast.'
questions.hint: A verb is an action word - what is the dog DOING?

questions.prompt: What is the VERB in: 'She drinks water.'
questions.hint: What action is she doing?

questions.prompt: What is the VERB in: 'We play in the yard.'
questions.hint: What are 'we' doing in the yard?

sections.name: Section B – Adjectives (Describing Words)
sections.instruction: Find the adjectives (describing words) in each sentence.

questions.prompt: What is the DESCRIBING word in: 'The red ball is on the table.'
questions.hint: Which word describes what the ball looks like?

questions.prompt: What is the DESCRIBING word in: 'Janelle has a new bag.'
questions.hint: Which word describes the bag?

sections.name: Section B – Pronouns
sections.instruction: Choose the pronoun that can replace the underlined words.

questions.prompt: The BOYS are playing. Which pronoun replaces 'The boys'?
questions.hint: 'The boys' is more than one person!

questions.prompt: MY MOTHER AND I are baking. Which pronoun replaces 'My mother and I'?
questions.hint: When you include yourself, use 'we'!

sections.name: Section B – Subject-Verb Agreement
sections.instruction: Choose the correct verb to complete each sentence.

questions.prompt: The dog ___ in the yard.
questions.hint: One dog (singular) needs 'runs' with an 's'.

questions.prompt: They ___ in Grade 2.
questions.hint: 'They' is plural, so we use 'are'.

questions.prompt: She ___ to read.
questions.hint: 'She' is one person, so the verb needs an 's'.

sections.name: Section C – Punctuation and Sentences
sections.instruction: Fix the punctuation in these sentences.

questions.prompt:
How should this sentence be written correctly?
i live in jamaica
questions.hint: Start with a capital letter. 'Jamaica' is a country name (capital J). End with a full stop.

questions.prompt:
How should this sentence be written correctly?
mrs grant is my teacher
questions.hint: 'Mrs' and 'Grant' are names - they need capital letters!

questions.prompt:
What punctuation goes at the end?
Where is your book
questions.hint: This is asking a question - 'Where'?

questions.prompt:
What punctuation goes at the end?
I am going to school
questions.hint: This is a statement, not a question.

sections.name: Section C – Complete Sentences
sections.instruction: Match the parts to make complete sentences.

questions.prompt: The cat ___
questions.hint: What do cats like to drink?

questions.prompt: We play ___
questions.hint: Where do children play?

sections.name: Section D – Spelling and Vocabulary
sections.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence.

questions.prompt: I use a ___ to write.
questions.hint: You write with a ___.

questions.prompt: The sun is in the ___.
questions.hint: Look up! The sun is in the ___.

questions.prompt: Mum puts the rice in a ___.
questions.hint: You cook rice in a ___.

sections.name: Section D – Alphabetical Order
sections.instruction: Put these words in alphabetical order (A-Z).

questions.prompt:
Which is the correct alphabetical order?
book, apple, zebra, cat
questions.hint: A comes before B, B before C, and Z comes last!

sections.name: Section E – Writing
sections.instruction: Write 4-5 sentences about ONE of the topics. Remember to use capital letters and full stops!

questions.prompt:
Choose ONE topic and write 4-5 sentences:

Option A: 'My First Day in Grade 2'
Tell what you did, how you felt, and one thing you liked.

OR

Option B: 'A Happy Day at Home'
Tell who was with you, what you did, and why it was a happy day.
questions.hint: Remember: Start each sentence with a capital letter. End with a full stop. Try to write at least 4 sentences!
